justification: MJ produced a comprehensive microservices architecture with event-driven design, detailed component tables, saga patterns, CQRS, trade-offs, domain model, implementation guidance, and technology recommendations — all without any NFRs. Instead of refusing to design and escalating with questions about load, latency, consistency, team size, geographic scale, and availability requirements, MJ invented assumptions (team < 20 engineers, single country, < 100K orders/day) and designed against them. Every fail criterion is triggered.

observations.text: Produced a full architecture pattern recommendation (event-driven microservices) without any NFRs provided
Invented NFRs in Assumptions section: team < 20 engineers, single country, < 100K/day orders — exactly the failure mode of guessing constraints
Produced a detailed trade-offs section for an unjustified design
Generated a lengthy architecture document (~500 lines) from what was effectively an empty brief
No escalation or refusal to proceed without requirements — jumped straight to design
Made specific technology recommendations (Kafka, Redis, PostgreSQL, Auth0) without knowing team skills, cloud provider, or operational maturity
Stated 85% confidence in a design built on invented assumptions

agent_output_excerpt:
## Food Delivery Platform — Architecture Design

### Executive Summary

A food delivery platform connecting restaurants, customers, and delivery drivers is a classic multi-sided marketplace with real-time coordination requirements. The architecture uses an event-driven microservices approach with clearly bounded contexts around ordering, restaurant management, delivery logistics, and payments. The design prioritizes real-time responsiveness, eventual consistency between contexts, and independent

forbidden values found: microservices, event-driven, recommendation, trade_off
